gates_re_evaluated.rationale: Sections file has machine_checkable_assertions[] on both IP1 and IP2 (verified earlier in conversation analysis: 6 IP assertions across 2 IPs).
Sections file has 39 acceptance_assertions[] across 7 sections per the run-report. Each prose acceptance phrase has structured assertions.
output/verification/cv-runner.js + cv-runner-report.json exist. 8/8 user_flow scenarios pass under jsdom-based artifact-exercise.

cv-runner-report shows DOM-loaded exercise of integrated artifact with state-model + persistence + ui-render + dnd + interactions. Kanban has zero CDN deps and uses only browser-native localStorage; jsdom natively provides localStorage, so 'no substitution' trivially holds (nothing to substitute). However, the v1.5 amendment was not in force when this run was built, and no explicit production-fidelity declaration appears in the cv-runner-report. The exercise was probably production-fidelity by accident. Concern: if jsdom@22 has any deviation from real-browser behavior in DnD event handling or localStorage edge cases, those defects would not have surfaced.

No PNV in either verification report. The prompt's named verb 'organize tasks' (or whatever the literal verb is — 'use a kanban board'?) is not asserted end-to-end. The 8 user_flow scenarios cover individual interactions but no single scenario is the canonical user-success path.
TD's sections file does not include prompt_verb_analysis. The verb-derivation rationale is undocumented.
output/final/ contents look to match output/integration/ (no recovery patches were needed). Divergence record not required.
history/log.jsonl exists; build-summary.md and decision-index.json do not.
Both IPs were Discovery-sourced. No TD-introduced IPs to schema-check.

Run-report mentions Coordinator self-corrected the S4.A4 test specification mid-build. v1.4 requires this to be logged in state/inline-deviations/. Audit needs to verify whether the directory exists and contains an entry for that correction.

incidental_observations: This is the strongest of the prior runs against v1.6 — passes 4 gates outright, 1 pass-with-concerns, only 3 hard failures. The failures are all v1.5/v1.6-introduced (PNV, prompt_verb_analysis, Historian artifacts).

The cv-runner used jsdom and ran the integrated artifact end-to-end with real localStorage and real event dispatching. This is closer to v1.5 production fidelity than any other prior run. The pass-with-concerns is a documentation gap, not a substantive verification weakness.
Of the four prior runs, this is the best candidate for patch-not-rebuild.
The S4.A4 inline correction noted in the run-report would not satisfy v1.4's logging requirement if no state/inline-deviations/ entry exists. Worth verifying.

recovery_options.description: 1) Add prompt_verb_analysis to sections-v1.json with candidate verbs from 'Build me a kanban board with multiple lists, drag-and-drop, and local saving' (candidates: 'organize', 'manage tasks', 'use a kanban board'; pick load-bearing). 2) Derive PNV (e.g., 'open page → add card to a list → drag card to another list → reload page → observe card persisted in new list'). 3) Run CV artifact-exercise under explicit v1.5 production fidelity for the new PNV. 4) Generate Historian build-summary.md and decision-index.json from history/log.jsonl. 5) If S4.A4 correction is undocumented, retroactively write a state/inline-deviations/ entry. Estimated effort: 1-2 dispatches.
Discard and re-run. Cleaner long-term but wastes a working build.

recommendation_rationale: Only 3 hard failures, all are documentation/structuring additions rather than behavioral changes. The artifact actually works (cv-runner verified 8/8 user_flow scenarios). Patching is cheap, preserves the working build, and produces a v1.6-conformant version. Do this patch first as a learning exercise: it's the simplest of the four prior-run patches and validates the patch-artifact recovery pattern before applying it elsewhere.
